LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT DWIGHT MONSON'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THERE BEING NO COMPLIANCE WITH THE TENTATIVE RULING RULE, NO ARGUMENT WAS HEARD AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE PLAINTIFF CANNOT PREVAIL, AND, IN ANY EVENT, PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO THE REASONABLENESS OF DEFENDANT'S ARTIFICIAL IRRIGATION, AND CAUSATION OF SEEPAGE. OBJECTION TO HUNTSMAN DECLARATION IS OVERRULED. THE COURT DENIED DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO CSR #7489

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-04-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Robert W. Keaster of Charlston Revich & Chamberlin, LLP, 310-551-7000, appearing for defendant and moving party

No other appearances on this matter. There being no compliance with the tentative ruling rule, no argument was heard and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT DWIGHT MONSON'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant has failed to demonstrate plaintiff cannot prevail, and, in any event, plaintiff has raised a triable issue of material fact as to the reasonableness of defendant's artificial irrigation, and causation of seepage. Objection to Huntsman declaration is overruled. The Court denied defendant's request for a continuance. Prevailing party to prepare form of order. (301/PJB/xGW) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR 7489
